subject: IBS
course_description: Instruction in rhetorical composition, critical reading, reflection, and writing as recursive process. Across themed sections, students engage with diverse genres. Courses share some common assignments including research writing and final student-curated portfolio with reflection project.
course_notes: (Freshmen Only) (Writing Among the Trees: Atlanta is sometimes known as the "city in a forest." You'll be able to see why if you go up to special collections on the tenth floor of Woodruff Library, which offers the best view on campus: the city looks like a forest punctuated by occasional highrises. From the Wesley Hollies to Lullwater Preserve, Emory's campus itself is lush with trees. In this class we will navigate the new environment of college writing as well as the new arboreal environment freshmen encounter on campus. We will approach trees as embodied fellow organisms, literary figures, and subjects of scientific study. Readings for the course will reflect a wide range of disciplines. Major assignments for the course will include two essays, brief presentations, and a final informational sign. In addition to the other assignments for the course, students will select a real tree to build a "relationship" with, through small writing exercises, for the duration of the semester.)
